plan_metadata.name: Feature DA Add Action From OpenAPI Spec

steps.description: Select the "Declarative Agent – Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option in the "New Project" dropdown within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "No Action" option in the dropdown menu within the "Create Declarative Agent" popup in Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Click the "Default Folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dropdown at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Click the "Add Action" item under DEVELOPMENT in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit panel of Visual Studio Code, targeting the entry with tooltip "Add action in declarative agent."
Click the "Start with an OpenAPI Description Document" option in the "Add an Action" dialog within the Microsoft Visual Studio Code extension for Microsoft 365 Agents.
Click the input field labeled "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location or Open File..." in the command palette at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.

Click on the text input field labeled "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location" in the OpenAPI Description Document prompt within the Visual Studio Code extension interface for Microsoft 365 Agents.

Type 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/huimiu/api-spec-example/main/repair-service.yml' into the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document URL" input field in the OpenAPI Description Document dialog within Visual Studio Code, triggered after selecting "Add Action" from the DEVELOPMENT panel.
Press the Enter key to confirm the OpenAPI Description Document URL input (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/huimiu/api-spec-example/main/repair-service.yml) in the VS Code extension dialog.
Click the "Selected" dropdown within the "Select Operation(s) Copilot Can Interact with" dialog in Visual Studio Code, just above the OK button.
Click the blue "OK" button in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot Can Interact with" dialog within the Visual Studio Code extension interface to confirm the selected API operations.
Click the "manifest.json" file entry in the file selection dialog within the Visual Studio Code interface, under the "Select Teams manifest.json File" modal.
Click the "Proceed" button in the confirmation dialog of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Press the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P in Visual Studio Code to open the Command Palette, making it accessible directly from the Explorer sidebar with project files visible.
Type the text 'show notifications' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input field at the top of the application window.
Click the "Notifications: Show Notifications" command in the VS Code command palette to select and execute it.
@assertion "added to the project successfully" exists.

Click the "Add Action" button in the DEVELOPMENT section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar within Visual Studio Code, targeting the item highlighted in blue with a tooltip labeled "Add action in declarative agent."
Click the "Start with an OpenAPI Description Document" option in the "Add an Action" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents extension panel in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location or Open File..." input box in the OpenAPI Spec Document modal within Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the red crosshair.
Click the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location" input field in the OpenAPI Description Document dialog within Visual Studio Code, above the development actions sidebar.

Type the URL 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/huimiu/api-spec-example/main/repair-service.yml' into the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document URL" input field within the Visual Studio Code Microsoft 365 Agents extension during the Add Action workflow.
Press the Enter key to confirm the URL input (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/huimiu/api-spec-example/main/repair-service.yml) in the "OpenAPI Description Document" dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Toggle all checkboxes" button in the API operation selection popup at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the language selector (flag icon) in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot Can Interact with" pop-up within Visual Studio Code to change the interface language.
Click the "manifest.json" file entry in the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Add" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code, to proceed with modifying files based on the provided OpenAPI document.
Activate the Visual Studio Code command palette by pressing ctrl+shift+P in the Explorer view with the .env.dev file selected within the project.
Type 'show notifications' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top center of the interface to trigger command suggestions.
Click the "Notifications: Show Notifications" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette dropdown, located beneath the search bar after typing ">show notifications".
@assertion "added to the project successfully" exists.
Click the “Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit” icon in the Visual Studio Code sidebar to open the extension panel.
Click the "AppPackage" folder in the Explorer panel within Visual Studio Code to expand its contents.
Click the "apiSpecificationFile" item in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code under the workspace folder.
@assertion "ai-plugin.json" and "ai-plugin_1.json" exists under the folder "appPackage". "openapi.yaml" and "openapi_1.yaml" exists under the folder "apiSpecificationFile".
Click the "env" folder in the Explorer panel within Visual Studio Code, expanding its contents in the project "VSCAUSE_APP_VTAMB."
Click the ".env" file under the "env" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click on the AGENT_SCOPE=Shared value in the .env.dev file within the Visual Studio Code editor.
Press the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+H to open the "Find and Replace" panel in the VS Code editor while editing the .env.dev file in the workspace explorer.
Type the text 'shared' into the Find input box in the Visual Studio Code editor while viewing the .env.dev file.
Click the "Replace" button in the Find and Replace bar of Visual Studio Code's .env.dev editor, with the search keyword "shared" highlighted in the results.
Type text: 'personal' into the "Replace" input field in the Find and Replace toolbar at the top of the .env.dev file within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "shared" value in the AGENT_SCOPE variable within the .env.dev file in Visual Studio Code, highlighting the text in the editor for potential editing.
Press the Escape key to dismiss the in-file search results panel within the .env.dev file in Visual Studio Code.
Save the currently edited .env.dev file in Visual Studio Code by pressing Ctrl+S while the environment variables are being modified in the editor pane.

Click the ".env.dev" file tab in the Visual Studio Code editor to focus on the open environment configuration file, as indicated by the red crosshair and the highlighted tab with a file icon and language badge.
Trigger the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code by pressing ctrl+shift+P, while viewing the workspace in the Explorer sidebar.
Type 'provision' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field to search for the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command.
Click the “Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision” option in the Visual Studio Code command palette, located within a dark-themed interface, immediately beneath the search bar containing “>provision”.
Open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code by pressing ctrl+shift+P while the Explorer view is active.
Type the text "show notifications" into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input field at the top center of the interface.
Click the settings gear icon in the Visual Studio Code command palette to open palette options while searching for "show notifications".
@assertion notification "provision stage executed successfully" exist.
